Title: A BAHD-type acyltransferase concludes the biosynthetic pathway of non-bitter glycoalkaloids in ripe tomato fruit

Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Vacuolar transport-related; exports α-tomatine from vacuole to cytosol
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates relocation of α-tomatine from vacuole to cytosol, enabling its downstream conversion toward Esculeoside A during ripening
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supportive target to improve precursor availability for Esculeoside A formation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GORKY exports α-tomatine from vacuoles to the cytosol during fruit ripening. 2. This transport step enables α-tomatine to access downstream enzymes of the Esculeoside A pathway.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as background/context rather than experimentally characterized in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Esculeoside A biosynthetic pathway / steroidal glycoalkaloid detoxification pathway
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; conserved HXXXD/DFGWG-type BAHD motifs implied
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in green fruit and flowers; also high in red ripe fruit; weak in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in green fruit and flowers; also high in red ripe fruit; predominant in immature and mature green fruit across wild accessions
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxytomatine; also hydroxy-dehydrotomatine, hydroxytomatidine, hydroxysolamargine in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group from acetyl-CoA to hydroxylated steroidal glycoalkaloids, notably converting hydroxytomatine to acetoxytomatine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of downstream Esculeoside A intermediates and end product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing/core pathway step established; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Non-bitter, less toxic ripe-fruit glycoalkaloid; improved palatability and reduced anti-nutritional toxicity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes acetoxytomatine formation and enables conversion of bitter/toxic α-tomatine toward non-bitter less-toxic Esculeoside A; knockout abolishes acetoxy-SGAs and causes hydroxylated precursor buildup
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage; earlier GAME36-associated steps appear ethylene-independent
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate gene screening; recombinant enzyme assay; coupled assays with GAME31; steady-state kinetics; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; metabolite profiling; in vitro full-pathway reconstruction; transient reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for breeding or engineering tomatoes with reduced bitterness/toxicity and improved conversion of α-tomatine to Esculeoside A; suitable for pathway reconstitution and high-value SGA production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME36 (Solyc08g075210) was identified as the missing BAHD acyltransferase in the Esculeoside A pathway. 2. It acetylates hydroxytomatine to acetoxytomatine and can acylate several hydroxylated SGAs in vitro. 3. CRISPR knockout eliminated acetoxy-SGAs and caused accumulation of hydroxylated precursors in leaves, flowers, and fruit. 4. Together with GAME31, GAME40, and GAME5, it enables full reconstruction of Esculeoside A biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts specifically on hydroxylated SGAs, not core SGAs directly; prefers acetyl-CoA over butyryl-CoA; no freely reversible reaction detected; ortholog in S. pennellii is functional, whereas eggplant homolog tested was inactive on these substrates; evidence suggests a minor alternative late-ripening route to Esculeoside A independent of GAME36 main route
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout causes strong accumulation of bitter/toxic precursor SGAs in fruit; no growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA PRJNA307656; RNA-seq: PRJNA798612

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Esculeoside A biosynthetic pathway / steroidal glycoalkaloid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates α-tomatine at C-23 to form hydroxytomatine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for GAME36 step and supplies flux to Esculeoside A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates α-tomatine conversion toward Esculeoside A by producing hydroxytomatine precursor
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage; reported as ethylene-independent in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously characterized; used here in coupled enzyme assays and full pathway reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with GAME36/GAME40/GAME5 for pathway engineering toward Esculeoside A
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME31 catalyzes the first hydroxylation step from α-tomatine to hydroxytomatine. 2. Its product is the direct substrate for GAME36. 3. It was necessary for in vitro and in planta reconstruction of the full Esculeoside A pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background/companion pathway enzyme in this paper rather than the principal discovery

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Esculeoside A biosynthetic pathway / steroidal glycoalkaloid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates acetoxytomatine at C-27 to form acetoxy-hydroxytomatine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for final glycosylation to Esculeoside A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Carries penultimate hydroxylation step toward Esculeoside A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously characterized; used here in pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful component for multigene engineering of Esculeoside A production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME40 catalyzes the penultimate C-27 hydroxylation step. 2. It functions downstream of GAME36 in the validated sequence of reactions. 3. Inclusion of GAME40 enabled full reconstitution of Esculeoside A biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Companion pathway enzyme in this paper; not a newly discovered focal gene

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Esculeoside A biosynthetic pathway / steroidal glycoalkaloid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly upregulated at breaker stage; affected in ripening mutants
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates acetoxy-hydroxytomatine at the 27-O position to form Esculeoside A
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ripening-associated; expression affected in ripening mutants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously characterized; used here in pathway reconstruction and transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential terminal gene for engineering Esculeoside A accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME5 catalyzes the last glycosylation step producing Esculeoside A. 2. It acts downstream of GAME40 in the validated pathway order. 3. Co-expression with GAME31/GAME36/GAME40 generated Esculeoside A in reconstruction assays.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Companion pathway enzyme in this paper; not principal discovery
